title: .aieng CAD parameter edit
description: Patch structure that a CAD adapter (e.g. aieng_freecad_mcp) writes back when applying a single named-parameter mutation via cad.edit_parameter. The schema constrains adapter writeback only; it does not validate solver/mesh evidence or claim physical correctness.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this parameter-edit artifact.

edit_id.description: Stable identifier for the edit attempt.

feature_id.description: Target feature ID. Must resolve against graph/feature_graph.json.

parameter_name.description: Named parameter on the target feature (e.g. 'thickness', 'radius'). Topology-changing parameters must be refused.

new_value.description: Requested new parameter value. Type and unit depend on the parameter.

previous_value.description: Parameter value before the edit, as observed by the adapter. Required for honest staleness reporting; null only when the parameter did not previously exist.

unit.description: Unit string (e.g. 'mm'); null when dimensionless or unknown.

bounds.description: Adapter-reported parameter bounds used during preflight.

topology_changing.description: True if the requested edit would change topology (e.g. removes/adds a face). Must be refused by the adapter.

refusal_reason.description: Required when any of the above is false. Free-text honest explanation.

required.description: Parameter edits are always approval-gated. The adapter must not silently apply.

status.description: Approval lifecycle state. 'auto_approved_for_benchmark' is reserved for benchmark harnesses and must be reflected in provenance.

approver.description: Identifier of the human or harness that granted/denied approval.

decided_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of approval decision; null when status is 'pending'.

status.description: What happened after approval. 'refused' covers preflight rejections; 'not_attempted' covers denial; 'failed' covers adapter/kernel errors.

path.description: Relative path inside the .aieng package, e.g. 'geometry/modified_{edit_id}.step'.

atomic.description: True when the export was rewritten atomically (e.g. via tempfile + replace). Required for any 'succeeded' state.

stale_artifacts.description: Relative paths inside the .aieng package whose validity depends on the prior geometry and that the adapter has marked stale. Typical entries: 'simulation/cae_imports/source_solver_deck.inp', 'simulation/mesh/*', 'results/field_regions.json', 'results/field_summary.json'. Downstream evidence must not be advanced until these are refreshed by their respective producers.

warnings.description: Honest non-fatal observations (e.g. 'value is within bounds but at 95% of upper limit').

provenance.description: Adapter metadata so evidence can be traced.

approval_gated.description: Parameter edits cannot bypass approval. Const guard.

topology_unchanged.description: Phase 32 only allows non-topology-changing single-parameter edits.

no_solver_run.description: Editing a parameter never produces solver evidence by itself.

no_mesh_run.description: Editing a parameter never produces mesh evidence by itself.

$comment: Adapter must mark downstream evidence stale via stale_artifacts; AIENG core never executes the CAD kernel and only validates the structure of this writeback.
